agent_output:
{
  "integration_assessment": {
    "component_interactions": [
      { "from": "SQS Queue", "to": "Image Worker", "status": "broken", "issue": "Catastrophic throughput mismatch: ingress ~300 jobs/min vs processing capacity ~15-20 jobs/min. Queue will grow unboundedly at ~280 jobs/min deficit. Within 1 hour of peak load the queue will have ~16,800 backlogged messages." },
      { "from": "Image Worker", "to": "SQS (visibility timeout)", "status": "broken", "issue": "Visibility timeout of 30s is dangerously tight. A batch of 10 messages at 2-4s each = 20-40s processing time. When a batch hits the upper bound (40s), it exceeds the 30s visibility timeout. Messages will become visible again mid-processing, causing duplicate processing of jobs that are still in-flight." },
      { "from": "SQS Queue", "to": "Dead Letter Queue", "status": "broken", "issue": "No DLQ configured. Poison messages (corrupt images, unsupported formats, oversized files) will be retried infinitely, consuming worker capacity and amplifying the throughput deficit." }
    ],
    "contract_compliance": "SQS consumer contract is structurally sound (MaxNumberOfMessages=10 is valid) but operationally broken due to capacity and timeout misconfigurations.",
    "data_flow_integrity": "At risk of duplicate processing due to visibility timeout expiry. No idempotency mechanism mentioned to guard against re-processing the same image twice (could result in duplicate writes, double billing, or corrupted output)."
  },

  "observability_review": {
    "logging": { "coverage": "gaps", "gaps": [
      "No mention of logging queue depth or approximate age of oldest message — critical for detecting backlog",
      "No logging of message receive-to-complete latency (end-to-end job duration)",
      "No logging of duplicate message detection (same MessageId processed twice)",
      "No logging of visibility timeout extensions or near-misses"
    ]},
    "metrics": { "coverage": "insufficient — only 2 counters exist", "gaps": [
      "CRITICAL: No queue depth metric (ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible) — the team will not know the queue is backing up until users complain",
      "CRITICAL: No job processing duration histogram — cannot detect slow jobs approaching visibility timeout",
      "No metric for in-flight messages (ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible)",
      "No metric for approximate age of oldest message — the single best indicator of queue health",
      "No metric for duplicate messages received",
      "No error breakdown by failure type (OOM, timeout, corrupt image, upstream failure)",
      "No metric for batch size actually received vs requested (important for understanding throughput)"
    ]},
    "tracing": { "coverage": "not mentioned", "gaps": [
      "No distributed trace IDs linking the job producer to the worker consumer",
      "No trace spans for individual processing stages (download, process, upload)"
    ]},
    "debugging_capability": "Poor. An on-call engineer at 3am would see jobs_failed_total incrementing but would have no way to determine: (1) whether the queue is backing up, (2) which jobs are failing and why, (3) whether duplicates are being processed, or (4) how close jobs are to the visibility timeout boundary."
  },

  "resilience_assessment": {
    "failure_modes": [
      { "scenario": "Sustained peak load (300 jobs/min)", "handling": "unhandled", "recommendation": "MUST configure autoscaling. A single worker at 15-20 jobs/min needs at minimum 15-20 concurrent workers to handle 300 jobs/min. Use ECS/Fargate autoscaling based on ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible or consider SQS-triggered Lambda." },
      { "scenario": "Poison message (corrupt/malformed image)", "handling": "unhandled", "recommendation": "MUST configure a dead-letter queue with maxReceiveCount (e.g., 3). Without a DLQ, a single poison message consumes retry capacity forever." },
      { "scenario": "Batch processing exceeds 30s visibility timeout", "handling": "unhandled", "recommendation": "MUST either (a) increase visibility timeout to at least 60s (2x worst case), or (b) implement per-message heartbeating via ChangeMessageVisibility to extend the timeout during processing, or (c) reduce batch size. Option (b) is preferred for robustness." },
      { "scenario": "Worker process crashes", "handling": "partial", "recommendation": "SQS will re-deliver messages after visibility timeout (good), but single-instance means zero processing during downtime. Need at minimum 2 instances for availability, plus health-check-based restart." },
      { "scenario": "Duplicate message processing", "handling": "unhandled", "recommendation": "Implement idempotency keys (e.g., based on SQS MessageId or a job-level deduplication ID). Without this, visibility timeout expiry WILL cause duplicate work — double image processing, double storage writes, potential data corruption." },
      { "scenario": "Downstream storage failure (S3/DB unavailable)", "handling": "unhandled", "recommendation": "No mention of retry with backoff for downstream writes. A transient S3 failure would fail the job, and without DLQ + retry policy, behavior is undefined." },
      { "scenario": "Out-of-memory on large images", "handling": "unhandled", "recommendation": "Processing 10 images concurrently in a single thread/process — large images could exhaust memory. Add memory limits and job-level resource guards." }
    ],
    "retry_config": "Relies entirely on SQS native retry (infinite retries, no backoff, no maxReceiveCount). This is dangerous — failed messages retry immediately and indefinitely.",
    "timeout_config": "30s visibility timeout is insufficient for worst-case batch processing (10 × 4s = 40s). Must increase to at least 2x worst case or implement heartbeating.",
    "circuit_breakers": "None. If a downstream dependency (S3, database) is down, the worker will burn through messages, fail them all, and they will re-enter the queue creating a retry storm.",
    "graceful_degradation": "None. No backpressure mechanism, no rate limiting, no graceful shutdown handling (SIGTERM may leave messages in-flight without extending visibility)."
  },

  "operational_readiness": {
    "deployment_ready": false,
    "rollback_capable": false,
    "rollback_plan": "No rollback plan documented. Single worker instance means a bad deploy takes the only processor offline. Need blue-green or rolling deployment with health check gates.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "Critically insufficient. Two counters (processed/failed totals) provide no visibility into the most important operational signals: queue depth, processing latency, backlog age, and duplicate rate. The team will discover problems only when end-users report them.",
    "on_call_documentation": "Not mentioned. An on-call engineer would need: runbook for queue backlog, procedure for draining poison messages, steps to manually scale workers, and thresholds for when to page.",
    "new_infrastructure_required": {
      "new_env_vars": [],
      "new_services": [],
      "new_queues_topics": ["Dead-letter queue for failed messages (with associated redrive policy, maxReceiveCount=3)"],
      "config_changes": [
        "SQS visibility timeout: increase from 30s to at least 60-90s",
        "Autoscaling policy based on queue depth (target: ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible < 100)",
        "SQS redrive policy pointing to DLQ with maxReceiveCount=3"
      ],
      "infra_provisioning": [
        "Minimum 15-20 worker instances (or equivalent autoscaling capacity) to handle 300 jobs/min peak load",
        "CloudWatch alarms on queue depth, oldest message age, and DLQ message count",
        "DLQ with its own monitoring and alerting"
      ]
    },
    "database_safety": {
      "has_migrations": false,
      "reversible": false,
      "data_preserving": false,
      "large_table_impact": false,
      "details": "No database migrations mentioned. Primary data safety risk is duplicate image processing from visibility timeout expiry — could result in duplicate records or overwritten outputs."
    },
    "verdict": "NOT READY"
  },

  "escalations": [
    { "type": "infrastructure_gap", "issue": "15-20x throughput deficit at peak load with no autoscaling", "reason": "Worker processes 15-20 jobs/min against 300 jobs/min ingress. Queue will grow by ~280 messages/min. Within one hour of peak load, backlog reaches ~16,800 messages. This is not a marginal gap — the system is architected for 1/15th of the required capacity.", "routed_to": "MJ (architect) + user (infrastructure provisioning decision)" },
    { "type": "infrastructure_gap", "issue": "No dead-letter queue configured", "reason": "Poison messages will retry infinitely, consuming worker capacity. Combined with the existing throughput deficit, even a single poison message further degrades an already critically under-provisioned system.", "routed_to": "Shaq (implement DLQ + redrive policy)" },
    { "type": "infrastructure_gap", "issue": "Visibility timeout will cause duplicate processing", "reason": "Batch of 10 messages × 4s worst case = 40s, exceeding 30s visibility timeout. Messages will be re-delivered while still being processed. No idempotency guard exists. This WILL cause duplicate work in production — it is a mathematical certainty, not a theoretical risk.", "routed_to": "Shaq (implement heartbeating or increase timeout + add idempotency)" },
    { "type": "missing_observability", "issue": "No queue depth, latency, or backlog age metrics", "reason": "The two existing counters (processed/failed totals) cannot detect the most critical failure mode: unbounded queue growth. The team will have zero advance warning of the backlog. By the time users report delays, the queue could contain tens of thousands of messages.", "routed_to": "Shaq (add CloudWatch metrics for queue depth, message age, processing duration)" }
  ],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 95,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Throughput math is deterministic: 300 in vs 15-20 out = system failure under peak load",
      "Visibility timeout breach is mathematically certain for 10-message batches at upper processing bound",
      "Absence of DLQ is a stated fact with well-understood consequences"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Actual processing parallelism within the single-threaded worker — 'some parallelism limited by single thread' is ambiguous, actual throughput may vary",
      "Whether downstream writes (S3, DB) have their own retry/idempotency mechanisms not described here"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Peak load of 300 jobs/min is a realistic production scenario, not a rare spike",
      "No external autoscaling mechanism (e.g., Kubernetes HPA) exists outside the described configuration",
      "The worker does not implement internal message-level visibility timeout extension (heartbeating)"
    ]
  }
}
